H-OS-L1.name: Pebble
H-OS-L1.sphere: n=96, mean=7.10, std=1.82, confidence=strong. Distribution is broad but centered high, with many 8‑10 scores. Qualitative (29) highlights 12 noticeable improvements, settling near 10 minutes, and strong music love. YouTube reach 29 views, no retention yet. AutoGluon predicts 6.488, lower than observed, indicating unrecognized potency.
H-OS-L1.sphere_plain: This overstimulation protocol brings strong relief, with most users feeling calmer within ten minutes and loving the rhythm.

H-OS-L2.name: Rainfall
H-OS-L2.sphere: n=52, mean=6.83, std=1.84, confidence=strong. Scores are slightly below H-OS-L1 but still strong, with moderate variance. Qualitative (21) notes 10 noticeable improvements, 5‑minute or 10‑minute settling, and music liked by many. YouTube views 5, no retention. AutoGluon predicts 6.488, again below observed.
H-OS-L2.sphere_plain: It reduces overstimulation well, offering steady calm that many appreciate despite a slight learning curve in rhythm.

H-OS-L3.name: Glacier
H-OS-L3.sphere: n=47, mean=7.04, std=1.68, confidence=meaningful. Distribution tight around high values, with many 8‑10 scores. Qualitative (23) reports 10 noticeable improvements, 5‑minute settlement, balanced rhythm. YouTube views 8, no retention. AutoGluon predicts 6.488, underestimates observed efficacy.
H-OS-L3.sphere_plain: Designed for overwhelming states, this protocol helps many settle quickly, with positive feedback on its calming rhythm.

H-AX-L1.name: Meadow
H-AX-L1.sphere: n=67, mean=7.22, std=1.93, confidence=strong. The response distribution is bimodal with a cluster of high scores near 9–10 and a tail below 3. Qualitative feedback from 23 listeners reports rapid settlement around 5–10 minutes and balanced, calming rhythm. YouTube reach is low (5 views) with no retention data yet. AutoGluon predicts a higher score (7.596), indicating possible underexposure.
H-AX-L1.sphere_plain: Listeners with anxiety often feel a noticeable calm after using this protocol, with many reporting improvement within about ten minutes.

H-AX-L2.name: Cottage
H-AX-L2.sphere: n=48, mean=6.94, std=1.62, confidence=meaningful. Distribution is tighter than L1 with fewer extreme high scores. Qualitative data from 20 listeners shows moderate improvement, 5‑minute settlement for many, and generally positive rhythm perception, though some neutrality remains. YouTube shows 6 views, still minimal. AutoGluon predicts 7.596, suggesting latent efficacy not fully realized.
H-AX-L2.sphere_plain: This protocol helps many people manage anxiety steadily, offering a gentle shift that many notice within five minutes.

H-AX-L3.name: Valley
H-AX-L3.sphere: n=36, mean=7.08, std=1.89, confidence=meaningful. Distribution shows right‑skewed pattern with several 9–10 scores. Qualitative responses (20) indicate longer settlement (up to end of session) and strong calming rhythm, with mixed music opinions. YouTube views are 4, no retention info. AutoGluon predicts 7.596, aligning closely with observed.
H-AX-L3.sphere_plain: People experiencing anxiety find this version effective, often settling into calm by the end of the session with a soothing rhythm.

H-CR-L1.name: Anchor
H-CR-L1.sphere: n=43, mean=6.35, std=1.70, confidence=meaningful. Scores are structurally low for crisis protocols but show a subset of high improvements. H-CR-L1.sphere_plain: In crisis moments, this protocol provides a steady grounding effect, helping many achieve noticeable relief within minutes.

H-CR-L2.name: Harbor
H-CR-L2.sphere: n=35, mean=6.40, std=1.72, confidence=meaningful. Distribution similar to L1 with modest variance. Qualitative (16) indicates moderate improvement and 5‑minute settling for some, with music split between neutral and liked. YouTube views 4, no retention. H-CR-L3.name: Horizon

H-SL-L1.name: Lantern

H-SL-L3.name: Midnight

sphere_commentary.overview:
The aggregated listener outcomes reveal distinct efficacy patterns across the Drum Protocol series, with HEALING showing highest consistency, THRIVING achieving peak scores, and TRANSFORMING displaying variable performance.
• HEALING protocols achieve mean 6.95 with tight variance, confirming robust entrainment across entry points.
• THRIVING protocols peak at L3 levels, with highest means (up to 7.56) and strong predicted scores.
• TRANSFORMING series shows lower means and higher spread, indicating challenges in ascending‑arc designs.
sphere_commentary.cross_series:
Across the three series, efficacy aligns with design intent, though response variability differs by series and protocol tier.
• HEALING maintains lowest variance, reflecting its calming DESCENT structure.
• THRIVING’s HOLD protocols, especially T-FC-L3, exceed observed means and align with AutoGluon predictions.
• TRANSFORMING shows the greatest variance and underperforms relative to model forecasts.
sphere_commentary.anomalies:
Several protocols exhibit statistical or contextual anomalies requiring investigation.
• X-MC-L3 (n=9) shows inverted distribution, low mean and negative qualitative feedback.
• X-MD-L3 displays bimodal outcomes and high variance, suggesting population mismatch.
• H-CR-L1 through H-CR-L3 show structurally expected low means but high qualitative improvement rates, indicating effective acute response.
• Multiple protocols exhibit noted embedding drift (sim < 0.85), implying evolving response patterns.
sphere_commentary.development_signals:
The analysis identifies several protocol refinements that align with observed patterns and model predictions.
• [CONVERGENT SIGNAL] X-MC-L3 — create L2 variant with shorter ASCENT to address bimodal underperformance.
• [CONVERGENT SIGNAL] T-FC-L3 — increase YouTube exposure via Adviser routing to capitalize on high efficacy.
• [CONVERGENT SIGNAL] X-ME-L1 — evaluate ASCENT parameter adjustments for low‑affect listeners.
• H-SL-L2 — redesign rhythm parameters after negative music opinions despite strong outcomes.
• X-MD-L3 — conduct subgroup analysis for motivation protocols to refine targeting.
sphere_commentary.embedding_analysis:
Embedding analysis reveals evolving patterns and analytical relationships across protocols.
• CROSS-RUN DRIFT: notable drift (sim < 0.85) observed in T-MB-L3, H-SL-L1, and several others, indicating shifting response profiles.
• CROSS-PROTOCOL SEMANTIC CLUSTERS: X-ME-L1 groups tightly with X-ME-L3; H-SL-L2 and H-CR-L2 emerge as analytically isolated.
• CONFIDENCE: Limited historical runs restrict stability of drift metrics, but patterns are consistent with emerging data.
